annotations.label: Common logarithm

anchor.snippet: the common logarithm (aka "standard logarithm") is the logarithm with base 10

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the general base-b logarithm `Real.logb`; the common logarithm is the specialization `Real.logb 10`, with no dedicated `log10` wrapper.

annotations.label: Mantissa property of base-10 logarithms

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of numbers greater than 1 that differ by a factor of a power of 10 all have the same fractional part

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Real.logb_mul` and `Real.logb_pow` from which `logb 10 (10^k * x) = k + logb 10 x` follows, but the fractional-part invariance is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Mantissa

anchor.snippet: The fractional part is known as the mantissa

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of the mantissa of a base-10 logarithm; one would form `Int.fract (Real.logb 10 x)` ad hoc.

annotations.label: Characteristic

anchor.snippet: The integer part, called the characteristic , can be computed by simply counting how many places the decimal point must be moved

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the characteristic (integer part) of a base-10 logarithm as a named concept.

annotations.label: Logarithm of 120

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of 120 is given by the following calculation

annotations.note: Concrete numeric demonstration; no specific worked-example lemma exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Negative logarithms of numbers less than 1

anchor.snippet: Positive numbers less than 1 have negative logarithms

annotations.note: `Real.logb_neg` states that for `1 < b` and `0 < x < 1`, `Real.logb b x < 0`, generalizing the base-10 claim.

annotations.label: Bar notation

anchor.snippet: a special notation, called bar notation, is used

annotations.note: Bar notation is a historical typographic convention for log tables; Mathlib has no such formalization.

annotations.label: Bar notation calculation of 0.012 × 0.85

anchor.snippet: The following example uses the bar notation to calculate 0.012 × 0.85 = 0.0102

annotations.note: Worked numeric example with a non-formalized notation; not represented in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mantissa invariance across powers of ten

anchor.snippet: Note that the mantissa is common to all of the 5 × 10 i

annotations.note: Derivable from `Real.logb_mul` and `Real.logb_pow`/`Real.logb_zpow`, but the explicit mantissa-invariance statement is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Numeric value identities for log base 10

anchor.snippet: The numerical value for logarithm to the base 10 can be calculated with the following identities

annotations.note: `Real.log_div_log` gives `Real.log x / Real.log b = Real.logb b x`, the change-of-base identity that underlies computing `log₁₀` from natural log.

annotations.label: Derivative of a logarithm

anchor.snippet: The derivative of a logarithm with a base b is such that

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Real.deriv_log` (`deriv Real.log x = x⁻¹`) but no dedicated derivative lemma for `Real.logb`; the base-b form must be obtained by combining `Real.log_div_log` with `Real.deriv_log`.
